Reviews Contact
Home / Services / Tile Flooring

Tile Flooring

Durable ceramic and porcelain for wet areas and high-traffic zones.

From Westerville mudrooms to Clintonville kitchen backsplashes, we install tile that stands up to Midwest weather. Our freeze-thaw rated porcelain resists cracking when temperatures swing from 90°F to -10°F. For homes with radiant heat, we use flexible thinset mortars that won't crack when systems activate.

Special techniques include slope grading for shower floors and anti-fracture membranes under large-format tiles. We source locally made tiles from Ohio manufacturers when possible, including replicas of original 1900s subway tile patterns found in Short North lofts.

All grout receives penetrating sealers that prevent staining from road salt and winter slush. We coordinate with plumbers to ensure proper waterproofing behind wet wall installations.

Our Process

  1. 01 Step1: Structural deflection testing
  2. 02 Step2: Crack isolation membrane application
  3. 03 Step3: Laser-leveled layout
  4. 04 Step4: Epoxy grout installation

Benefits

  • Withstands heavy entryway traffic
  • Freeze-thaw stable
  • Historic pattern matching
  • Easy winter cleanup

Frequently Asked Questions

What tile works best for my sunroom addition?

We recommend through-body porcelain that won't fade in south-facing rooms. Darker grout hides dirt from patio access.

Can you match my 1920s hex tile?

Our suppliers carry reproduction hex tiles with authentic color variations. We can blend new installations seamlessly.

Get a Free Estimate

Ready to get started with tile flooring? Contact us today.

Request Quote Or call: 555-flo-r91
